content.text:
# Ingest Statistical-Learning course

You are ingesting a new course (`Statistical-Learning`) into an existing Obsidian vault. The vault has a fully specified workflow at `School/CLAUDE.md` — read it first and follow it. This prompt only adds course-specific orientation and sequencing on top of that workflow.

## Phase 0 — Setup (do this before anything else)

### 0.1 Read the rulebook

Read `School/CLAUDE.md` end to end. Pay special attention to:
- The slide ingest workflow
- The mock-exam and quiz ingest workflow
- The "search before create" rule for `20-Concepts/`
- The diagram rules (Mermaid first, image allowlist)
- The citation format (`[[…#page=N|slide N]]` wikilinks, exact filenames including `(1)` suffixes)
- The shape of an exam blueprint

### 0.2 Generate slide-PNGs from the PDFs

`bin/ingest-slides.sh` expects `.pptx` source files. This course has only PDFs. You must produce the `slides-png/{lecture-name}/slide-NN.png` layout the rest of the workflow expects, by rasterizing each PDF directly.

Run this from the vault root. It uses `pdftoppm` (ships with poppler — installed on most Macs; if missing, `brew install poppler`).

```bash
COURSE="Statistical-Learning"
SRC="School/30-Sources/$COURSE/pdf"
DST="School/30-Sources/$COURSE/slides-png"
mkdir -p "$DST"
for pdf in "$SRC"/*.pdf; do
  name="$(basename "$pdf" .pdf)"
  out="$DST/$name"
  mkdir -p "$out"
  pdftoppm -png -r 120 "$pdf" "$out/slide" -f 1
  # rename slide-1.png → slide-01.png etc. for natural sort
  cd "$out" && for f in slide-*.png; do
    n="${f#slide-}"; n="${n%.png}"
    new=$(printf "slide-%02d.png" "$n")
    [ "$f" != "$new" ] && mv "$f" "$new"
  done
  cd - >/dev/null
done
```

Verify by listing one directory: `ls School/30-Sources/Statistical-Learning/slides-png/SLP-Lec1-knn\(1\)/ | head`. You should see `slide-01.png`, `slide-02.png`, etc.

The lecture-name folder under `slides-png/` matches the PDF filename without `.pdf` — including any `(1)` suffix and any typos. Do not rename. The CLAUDE.md citation rules require exact filenames.

### 0.3 Read the syllabus

`School/30-Sources/Statistical-Learning/syllabus/SyllabusPDF.pdf`. Extract:
- Semester and exam date (for the manifest)
- Exam format and marks distribution (for the manifest's `exam-format` field — preliminary, the mock exam will refine it)
- Lecture order and topic mapping (critical — see "filename gotchas" below)
- Prof's name, grading conventions, anything quirky

### 0.4 Create the course manifest

Copy `School/99-Templates/_course.md` to `School/30-Sources/Statistical-Learning/_course.md`. Fill in everything you can infer from the syllabus and the file inventory. If `semester` or `exam-date` aren't recoverable from the syllabus, leave them as `TBD` — do not block on them.

For `display-name`, use `Statistical Learning` (without the dash). For `course-code`, use `Statistical-Learning` exactly (must match the folder).

Suggest a `diagram-categories` palette covering the course's recurring diagram types (e.g. linear models, tree-based, kernel methods, dim-reduction, neural). 4–6 categories is plenty.

### 0.5 Resolve filename → lecture-number mapping

The PDFs in `30-Sources/Statistical-Learning/pdf/` are named inconsistently:

```
SLP-Lec1-knn(1).pdf
SLP-lec2(1).pdf
SLP-lec3(1).pdf
SLP-04(1).pdf
Lec-05-backprop(1).pdf
Lec-06-improving-MLPs(1).pdf
Lec7-slides.pdf
SLP-Loss-functions-regs.pdf
SLP-Bias-variance(1).pdf
SLP-SVMs-I.pdf
SLP-Kernels(1).pdf
SLP-Kernels-II(1).pdf
SLP-PCA.pdf
SLP-DimRed-II(1).pdf
dtrees_slides_SLP.pdf
example-dtree.pdf
kmeans_slides.pdf
SLP-Bagging.pdf
SLP-Boosting.pdf
SLP-Adaboost(1).pdf
```

Some have explicit lecture numbers, several don't. **Use the syllabus to determine the canonical lecture order.** Build a mapping table — write it into `_course.md` under a `## Lecture mapping` section so future-you can find it. Format:

| Lecture # | Topic | PDF filename |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 01 | k-NN | SLP-Lec1-knn(1).pdf |
| 02 | … | SLP-lec2(1).pdf |
| … | … | … |

If the syllabus is silent on a deck, infer from the topic (e.g. Bagging → Boosting → AdaBoost is the standard ensemble sequence) and flag the inference in the table.

**`example-dtree.pdf` is almost certainly a worked example, not a lecture.** Don't create a lecture note for it. Treat it as supporting material — link to it from the decision-trees lecture note under a `## Worked examples` section, citing it with a wikilink.

## Phase 1 — Mock exam → blueprint

Per the mock-exam workflow in CLAUDE.md.

1. Read `School/30-Sources/Statistical-Learning/mock-exams/mock_exam_spring26_BCSAI_SLP.pdf` in full.
2. Create `School/40-Reviews/Statistical-Learning/exam-blueprint.md` following the schema in CLAUDE.md exactly — frontmatter with `sources:`, then sections in order: Format, Marks distribution, Recurring patterns, Topic coverage map, Prof tells, What the formula sheet provides, What the formula sheet does NOT provide.
3. The Topic coverage map is the weighting signal you'll use in Phase 2 — be thorough. For every question in the mock, note (a) the topic, (b) which lecture covers it, (c) the question shape (MCQ definition / MCQ contrast / exercise / proof / code / etc.).
4. Surface gaps: any topic tested in the mock that has no concept note in `20-Concepts/` yet. List them at the bottom of the blueprint under `## Gaps to fill during lecture ingest`. Don't create the concept notes yet — they'll get created naturally during Phase 2 when their parent lecture is ingested.

## Phase 2 — Lecture ingest, all decks

Per the slide ingest workflow in CLAUDE.md, applied to every PDF in lecture order (per the mapping you built in 0.5). Do them in numeric order — earlier lectures' concepts get referenced by later ones.

For each lecture:

1. Create `School/10-Courses/Statistical-Learning/lectures/lecture-NN-{topic-slug}.md` with the full frontmatter spec from CLAUDE.md.
2. Read the PDF + spot-check slide PNGs to verify slide numbers when citing.
3. Extract atomic concepts to `20-Concepts/`. **Apply search-before-create rigorously** — many concepts will already exist from the NLP course. Examples likely to overlap: `backpropagation`, `support-vector-machine`, `softmax`, `cross-entropy`, `multilayer-perceptron`, `perceptron`, `activation-function`, possibly `latent-semantic-analysis` (related to PCA). For each:
   - If a match exists, add `Statistical-Learning` to `courses:`, append the new `sources:` entry, and extend the body with this course's framing if it differs meaningfully. Do not duplicate.
   - If no match, create a new note per the standard concept template.
4. Generate flashcards in `40-Reviews/Statistical-Learning/flashcards/lecture-NN.md`, multi-line `?` format, 15–25 cards, atomic recall, exam-oriented.

### Course-specific search-before-create flags

Before creating any of these as new concept notes, **glob `20-Concepts/` first** and check for existing entries:

| Concept likely in this course | Likely existing slug(s) to check |
| --- | --- |
| Backpropagation | `backpropagation.md` |
| Multilayer perceptron / MLP | `multilayer-perceptron.md` |
| SVMs | `support-vector-machine.md` |
| Softmax | `softmax.md` |
| Cross-entropy loss | `cross-entropy.md` |
| Activation functions | `activation-function.md` |
| Sigmoid | `sigmoid.md` |
| Perceptron | `perceptron.md` |
| Bayes / probabilistic | `bayes-formula.md`, `naive-bayes.md` |
| PCA | check `latent-semantic-analysis.md` (related but distinct — probably create a new `pca.md` and cross-link, since PCA and LSA are not the same concept even though LSA uses SVD on a term-document matrix) |
| Regularization, bias-variance | new — likely no existing notes |
| Decision trees, k-NN, k-means | new |
| Bagging, boosting, AdaBoost | new |
| Kernels, kernel trick | new |

If you find any other overlaps during ingest, apply the same rule — do not duplicate.

### Diagram expectations for this course

Several SLP topics are inherently visual and will pull on the diagram rules in CLAUDE.md:

- **Decision trees** — Mermaid `flowchart TD` works perfectly. Don't embed slide images of trees.
- **k-means / clustering** — final cluster assignments on real data may justify an image embed (visualization allowlist). Iteration steps as Mermaid.
- **PCA / dimensionality reduction** — projection onto principal components on real data may justify an image (plotted graph allowlist). The algorithm itself is Mermaid.
- **SVM margins, kernels** — geometry on real data may justify an image. Algorithm flow as Mermaid.
- **Bias-variance trade-off** — the canonical curve plot is on the allowlist (plotted graph with specific shape). Embed the slide PNG.
- **Bagging / boosting / AdaBoost** — ensemble flow as Mermaid (`flowchart LR`, parallel for bagging, sequential for boosting).
- **MLP / backprop** — architecture as Mermaid; computational graph for backprop also as Mermaid.

When in doubt, default to Mermaid per CLAUDE.md.

## Phase 3 — Reconcile

After every lecture has been ingested:

1. Re-read the blueprint's `## Gaps to fill during lecture ingest` section. Verify every gap is now closed (concept note exists). If anything is still open, either it wasn't covered in any lecture (flag it back to me) or you missed it during ingest (create it now from the mock exam alone).
2. Update the blueprint's `## Topic coverage map` to include the lecture-note links (`[[lecture-NN-…]]`) alongside the question references. Now the blueprint is fully cross-linked.
3. Print a summary to chat:
   - Number of lectures ingested
   - Number of new concept notes created
   - Number of existing concept notes extended (with `Statistical-Learning` added to `courses:`)
   - Any deviations from this prompt or CLAUDE.md, with reasoning
   - Any unresolved questions for me

## Approval gate

CLAUDE.md says: *"Show me a summary of what you'll create BEFORE writing any files. Wait for 'go'."*

Apply that rule **once per phase**, not per file — otherwise this becomes 20+ approval cycles. Specifically:

- **Before Phase 0.4** (manifest creation): show the manifest content you'll write + the lecture-mapping table. Wait for "go".
- **Before Phase 1** (blueprint): show the blueprint outline + topic coverage map skeleton. Wait for "go".
- **Before Phase 2** (lecture ingest): show the full ingest plan as a table — for each lecture, the lecture-note slug, the concepts to extract, which concepts will be new vs. extended. Wait for "go".
- **No per-lecture approval** during Phase 2 once "go" is given. Just execute.
- **At Phase 3**: print the summary, no approval needed.

Begin with Phase 0.

rawInput.description: List syllabus and mock exam files

entries.content: Phase 0.2: Generate slide PNGs from PDFs
Phase 0.4: Create course manifest with lecture mapping (approval gate)
Phase 1: Read mock exam, draft blueprint outline (approval gate)
Phase 2: Lecture ingest plan (approval gate), then execute all lectures

content.text: Starting Phase 0. Generating slide PNGs from PDFs first.

content.title:
COURSE="Statistical-Learning"
SRC="30-Sources/$COURSE/pdf"
DST="30-Sources/$COURSE/slides-png"
mkdir -p "$DST"
for pdf in "$SRC"/*.pdf; do
  name="$(basename "$pdf" .pdf)"
  out="$DST/$name"
  mkdir -p "$out"
  pdftoppm -png -r 120 "$pdf" "$out/slide" -f 1
  cd "$out" && for f in slide-*.png; do
    n="${f#slide-}"; n="${n%.png}"
    new=$(printf "slide-%02d.png" "$n")
    [ "$f" != "$new" ] && mv "$f" "$new"
  done
  cd - >/dev/null
done
echo "DONE"

content.text:
All 20 PDFs rasterized. Updating todos and showing the manifest plan for approval.## Phase 0 — approval gate: manifest + lecture mapping

**`30-Sources/Statistical-Learning/_course.md` will contain:**

```yaml
---
course-code: Statistical-Learning
display-name: Statistical Learning
semester: Spring 2026
exam-date: TBD                  # final exam is Session 30; specific date not in syllabus
exam-format: |
  TBD — preliminary: course has midterm (25%) + final (40%, must score ≥3.5/10 to pass).
  Will be refined after mock-exam ingest in Phase 1.
diagram-categories:
  linear-models: "#5b8def"      # perceptron, linear classifiers, SVMs
  neural: "#e36f6f"             # MLP, backprop, deep learning
  trees-ensembles: "#6fb86f"    # decision trees, bagging, boosting, AdaBoost
  kernels: "#c98ae0"            # kernel trick, kernel methods
  clustering: "#e6a85c"         # k-means, hierarchical
  dim-reduction: "#5fb6b6"      # PCA, dim-red II
---

# Statistical Learning

## Course quirks
- Course code AISLP-CSAI.3.M.B; titled "AI: Statistical Learning & Prediction" (BCSAI, 3rd year, semester 2).
- Instructor: **Luciano Dyballa** (Yale PhD, ML/vision/comp-neuro). Decks credit J. Johnson (UMich) and Li/Johnson/Yeung (Stanford) — heavily inspired by EECS 498/CS231n.
- Quizzes given throughout the semester; midterm is Session 8, final is Session 30.
- AI tools forbidden in quizzes/exams.
- Pass requires ≥3.5/10 on the final exam regardless of other grades.

## Sources at hand
- [x] Slides (`pdf/`, `slides-png/` — 20 decks rasterized)
- [ ] Notebooks
- [x] Mock exams (`mock-exams/mock_exam_spring26_BCSAI_SLP.pdf`)
- [ ] Quizzes
- [x] Syllabus (`syllabus/SyllabusPDF.pdf`)

## Lecture mapping

The professor's PDFs include explicit "Lecture N" labels for decks 1–7. Post-midterm decks aren't numbered; I've assigned consecutive numbers in the syllabus order.

| Lecture # | Topic | PDF filename |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 01 | k-NN, curse of dimensionality | SLP-Lec1-knn(1).pdf |
| 02 | Linear classifiers, the perceptron | SLP-lec2(1).pdf |
| 03 | Intro to neural nets | SLP-lec3(1).pdf |
| 04 | Multi-layer perceptrons | SLP-04(1).pdf |
| 05 | Backpropagation | Lec-05-backprop(1).pdf |
| 06 | Improving MLPs (vanishing/exploding gradients, activations) | Lec-06-improving-MLPs(1).pdf |
| 07 | Learning-rate schedules / training deep nets | Lec7-slides.pdf |
| 08 | Decision trees | dtrees_slides_SLP.pdf |
| 09 | Linear SVMs | SLP-SVMs-I.pdf |
| 10 | Loss functions & regularizers | SLP-Loss-functions-regs.pdf |
| 11 | Bias–variance decomposition | SLP-Bias-variance(1).pdf |
| 12 | Ensemble methods I: Bagging | SLP-Bagging.pdf |
| 13 | Ensemble methods II: Boosting | SLP-Boosting.pdf |
| 14 | AdaBoost | SLP-Adaboost(1).pdf |
| 15 | Kernel methods I | SLP-Kernels(1).pdf |
| 16 | Kernel methods II | SLP-Kernels-II(1).pdf |
| 17 | Clustering (k-means) | kmeans_slides.pdf |
| 18 | PCA (dimensionality reduction I) | SLP-PCA.pdf |
| 19 | Dimensionality reduction II | SLP-DimRed-II(1).pdf |

`example-dtree.pdf` is a worked example, not a lecture — link it under a `## Worked examples` section in lecture 08 (decision trees).

**Notes on inferred numbering:**
- Decks 1–7 have explicit lecture numbers in their title slides → kept verbatim.
- Decks 8–19 numbered consecutively in syllabus topic order. The syllabus reserves Session 8 for midterm and Sessions 28–30 for presentations/final, so my "lecture N" doesn't exactly match the syllabus session N after lecture 7 — but the topic order matches.
- Bagging → Boosting → AdaBoost is the standard ensemble sequence; AdaBoost is the canonical specific instance of boosting, so I gave it its own lecture rather than folding it into 13.

**Reply "go" to write the manifest, or push back on the mapping/categories.**
